codeReview.summary: The feature adds 9 tests for cryptographic event chaining in the existing audit.rs module. The implementation correctly verifies prev_hash chaining, hash integrity, and tamper detection. No blocking issues found.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed AGENTS.md conventions correctly - scoped validation to swell-core, ran build/test/clippy/fmt checks, committed only the relevant file. No deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows only audit.rs was modified, all validators passed, proper scoping was used.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed Rust skill guidance appropriately. The transcript shows they discovered the existing audit module lacked tests matching the `audit_chain` filter, then correctly added a submodule that matches.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'I see the issue - the existing tests in audit.rs don't match the `audit_chain` filter. I need to add tests that will be matched by `cargo test -p swell-core -- audit_chain`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly identified that the existing `verify_chain()` method on `AuditLog` already implemented the cryptographic chaining logic. The tests added verify this existing implementation rather than adding new functionality.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff notes 'The existing audit.rs already had the full implementation with hash chaining, verify_chain(), and tamper detection - I added tests to match the verification filter.'

summary: Feature obs-audit-chain passes review. Worker correctly identified that the cryptographic audit chain implementation already existed in audit.rs (hash chaining via prev_hash, verify_chain(), tamper detection via SHA-256). The worker added 9 comprehensive tests that match the `cargo test audit_chain` filter. All expected behaviors are verified: prev_hash inclusion in each entry, chain integrity verification via hash recomputation, and tampering detection for both modified and missing entries. No blocking issues. Worker followed proper procedures, used correct validation scoping, and made no deviations from skill guidance or AGENTS.md conventions.
